matches.rationale: Same Edit action and README-path prerequisite; the regex narrows subdirectories to packages/ and crates/, which is partial coverage.
Same Write action and README-path prerequisite; the regex narrows subdirectories to packages/ and crates/, which is partial coverage.
Targets edits to Next.js source/tests and requires starting the same watch command first; its test/ scope is broader than integration tests.
Targets the filtered Next.js build and instructs using the types command when checking type errors only.
Same branch-checkout trigger and full bootstrap build requirement.
Same Write action for new test suites and requirement to generate them with pnpm new-test; the filename regex is narrower.
Same prohibition on rerunning a test suite for different grep analyses and same capture-once instruction; the command pattern is mode-script-specific.
Same Edit trigger for test waits and prohibition on setTimeout in favor of retry().
Same Edit trigger for deprecated check() and replacement with retry() plus expect().
Same Edit trigger for inline files objects in test fixture setup and preference for real fixture directories.
Same PR/CI-status investigation intent and instruction to use scripts/pr-status.js; the regex triggers on direct gh status commands.
Same local CI reproduction action and requirement to match CI mode and environment variables, especially IS_WEBPACK_TEST.
The extracted compound quick-fix rule includes the exact rust check/build trigger and cargo fmt sequence. Its Prettier clause is not separately matchable under 1:1.
Same PR-description creation action and mandatory NEXT_JS_LLM_PR marker at the bottom.
Same staging/commit action and prohibition on committing local secret files; the placeholder-documentation clause is omitted.
Same HTTPS remote mutation after SSH-auth trouble and same prohibition absent explicit user request.
Same force-push/rebase action in a partial-clone context and SSH preflight preference.
Same commit action and prohibition on Generated with Claude Code and co-author footers.
Same pre-commit validation requirement; the extraction omits the specific formatting/lint checks.
Same gh pr ready action and requirement to leave the PR in draft for the user.
Same integration-test action after source changes and prerequisite to rebuild.
Same Edit action for a new feature flag and the type/schema/define-env wiring; the regex covers only config-shared.ts, so coverage is partial.
Same define-env edit for edge builds and requirement to force Node-import-gating flags false.
Same Edit action prohibiting react-server-dom-webpack imports outside entry-base.ts and directing use of module exports.
Same Edit action in app-page.ts and prohibition on relative requires of internal modules.
Same Edit action involving browser.back() on router-act pages with visible accordion links.
Same Rust Edit action, preference against super:: imports, and inline-mod exception.
Same documentation Edit action for highlight attributes and requirement to count code-block lines accurately.
Same server-code Edit/review action for new nonstandard request-header reads and security-review flagging.

misses.note: No Write rule starts the watch process before creating Next.js source or integration-test files; only the Edit projection was extracted.
No extracted Bash rule requires keeping the watch process running during iteration.
No extracted Bash rule selects the focused test script matching both mode and bundler.
No extracted Bash rule terminates the background watch process when finished.
No extracted Bash rule prefers the filtered Next.js build for core-only edits after bootstrap.
No extracted Bash rule covers full build-all for bootstrap, pre-CI, or multi-package changes.
No extracted Bash rule requires --webpack for next build/dev when Webpack is desired.
Compiler reported as uncompilable: "There is no `--no-turbopack` flag (a fact, no obligation/prohibition attached)".
The compiler extracted only a Write rule for test creation, not the Bash rule requiring pnpm new-test to generate every test.
No extracted Bash rule enforces non-interactive pnpm new-test -- --args usage and argument format for AI agents.

Compiler reported this within: "General CI-triage prioritization heuristics: blocking failures (build/lint/types) before test failures, treat 'Known Flaky Tests' as context not an automatic dismissal reason, prioritize CI failures over review comments (general priority ordering, not one tool call)".

No extracted Bash rule invokes the referenced pr-status-triage skill for automated analysis.

Compiler reported this within: "General CI-triage prioritization heuristics: blocking failures (build/lint/types) before test failures, treat 'Known Flaky Tests' as context not an automatic dismissal reason, prioritize CI failures over review comments (general priority ordering, not one tool call)".
The only extracted quick-fix rule combines Rust and Prettier actions and was assigned to the closer first Rust clause under 1:1; there is no separate extracted Prettier rule.
No extracted Bash rule runs the specific failing test path locally.
No extracted Bash rule chooses the development/production script matching the failing mode.
No extracted Bash rule binds Next.js debug logging to DEBUG=next:*.
No extracted Bash rule sets NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 for local tests.
No extracted Edit rule binds development-bundle-only code to a NODE_ENV build-time guard.
No extracted Edit rule binds dev-server-only code to __NEXT_DEV_SERVER.

Compiler reported within: "General secrets hygiene: treat env values as sensitive, never paste secret values in chat/commit messages/shared logs, replicate CI env var names but not literal secret values, stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholder credentials when a required secret is missing locally (broad safety principles not tied to one specific tool call)".

unmatched_extracted.note: Real document rule requiring API output to be saved and reused rather than re-querying; no frozen gold rule lists it.
Real action-bindable rule requiring snapshot updates to use the exact CI environment flags; no frozen gold rule lists this narrower snapshot action.
Real action-bindable rule for exercising PPR paths with __NEXT_CACHE_COMPONENTS=true; no frozen gold rule lists it.
Covers additional runtime-internal wiring for the same new-feature-flag rule g50 already matched by new-flag-wiring-config-shared.
Real action-bindable DCE rule requiring require() calls behind compile-time if/else branches; no frozen gold rule lists it.
